title: Packages and access control deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Packages and access control; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Packages and access control: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Package-private members are private to one source file, and protected means accessible only by subclasses everywhere.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Package-private access is shared across the package; protected combines package access with constrained subtype access, so expose the minimum stable surface.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Package-private access is shared across the package; protected combines package access with constrained subtype access, so expose the minimum stable surface.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Package-private access is shared across the package; protected combines package access with constrained subtype access, so expose the minimum stable surface.
Package-private members are private to one source file, and protected means accessible only by subclasses everywhere.

answer.explanation: Package-private access is shared across the package; protected combines package access with constrained subtype access, so expose the minimum stable surface.
